Mandaluyong Office · Energy Efficiency Program
Meet Team Kidlat
The people responsible for driving energy efficiency and accountability across the Mandaluyong Office.
3
Floor Champions
2,200
kWh Monthly Target
6
Month Program
4
Daily Inspections
Champion Directory
Floor Champions Own Execution
Clear floor ownership turns policy into repeatable daily action. Each champion is accountable for their floor's energy performance.
Tammy Padal
Team Leader
3rd Floor Champion
Responsibilities
  • Regulate 3rd Floor AC
  • Update weekly performance
  • Lead Team Kidlat initiatives
  • Track AC maintenance schedules
Jason Templonuevo
2nd Floor Champion
Zone Discipline
Responsibilities
  • Regulate 2nd Floor AC
  • Consolidate active workspaces
  • Reinforce energy-saving habits
  • Monitor floor compliance
Edizon Veloria
1st Floor Champion
Office Custodian
Responsibilities
  • Daily meter readings
  • Perform inspections
  • Monitor AC and water cooler usage
  • Support reporting and compliance
01 / Objective
Team Kidlat Sets the Standard
Team Kidlat is the designated enforcement and monitoring unit for the Mandaluyong Office. The team builds awareness through visible routines, floor-level accountability, and shared reporting.
🔒
Enforce Habits
Champion disciplined use of AC, PCs, active zones, and shared spaces throughout the day.
📊
Monitor Data
Support daily readings, inspection rounds, and front-desk performance tracking.
📢
Publish Results
Keep the six-month program visible through monthly results and progress against the 2,200 kWh target.
02 / Daily Routine
Results Must Stay Public
The six-month program depends on consistent inspection, reporting, and visible monthly results.
AM
Morning Reading
Edizon records the meter reading at the start of every day.
4×
Periodic Rounds
Floor inspections at 10 AM, 12 PM, 3 PM, and 5 PM daily.
GC
Real-Time Reporting
Status updates and reminders sent through the office group chat.
FD
Front-Desk Visibility
Tammy updates the display showing yesterday's performance vs weekly trend.
⚡
Consumption → 2,200 kWh
Monthly consumption trending toward the 2,200 kWh billing target.
✅
100% Reporting Consistency
Daily meter reports and front-desk updates completed without gaps.
📉
Reduced Idle Energy
Unneeded equipment in unoccupied zones visibly declines over time.
📅
Monthly Publication
Results published every month to keep all employees aware and accountable.
03 / Team Activities
Team Kidlat Activities
Document inspection rounds, meter readings, team meetings, and front-desk monitoring.
📸
Upload Activity Photos
Click to add photos · Inspection rounds, meter readings, team meetings
Mandaluyong Office · Effectivity May 2026
Energy Efficiency Policy
Employee discussion briefing for disciplined office energy habits. The target is measurable, visible, and shared.
2,200
kWh Target
3,100
Current kWh
29%
Reduction Needed
01 / Target
The Target Is 2,200 kWh
EasyPC Mandaluyong's policy converts a monthly reduction target into an employee-level operating discipline.
Current Monthly Use
3,100 kWh
→
Reduction needed29%
900 kWh to cut · ₱14,859 savings at current rate
Target Monthly Use
2,200 kWh
📏
Measurable
The target is tracked daily through meter readings and published for all employees to see.
👁
Visible
Front-desk display shows yesterday's result vs the weekly trend every single day.
🤝
Shared
Every employee contributes. Energy saving is a core professional accountability — not optional.
02 / Habits
Daily Habits Drive the Reduction
The policy depends on repeated, practical actions employees can apply throughout the workday.
🔌
Switch Off Idle Equipment
Turn off PCs, monitors, chargers, lights, and unused devices when not actively needed.
❄️
Hold AC at 22°C
Keep air-conditioning at the standard temperature. Avoid unnecessary thermostat adjustments.
👕
Dress for the Office Climate
Wear comfortable attire suited to 22°C so efficiency and comfort work together.
🏢
Consolidate Active Zones
Use communal spaces where practical to reduce cooling and lighting demand across floors.
Professional Accountability
Energy saving is part of disciplined office conduct. Everyone contributes by preventing idle energy and using space efficiently.
03 / Visibility
Visibility Keeps Everyone Aligned
Daily performance tracking turns the policy from a reminder into a shared operating system.
1
Track Yesterday's Consumption
The front-desk display shows the latest result so employees see the current performance signal.
2
Compare Against Weekly Trend
Consumption is checked against the weekly pacing target to identify overuse early.
3
Correct Immediately
If results exceed plan, teams act the same day through reminders, equipment checks, and space consolidation.
Weekly Pacing Target
550 kWh
Keeps the office on track toward the monthly 2,200 kWh objective.
Weekly target550 kWh
4 weeks × 550 kWh = 2,200 kWh/month
Visible results create pressure to improve.
When performance is public, energy saving becomes part of everyday office awareness.
Source: EasyPC Energy Efficiency Program briefer, Mandaluyong Office, May 2026.
